32.gl: to ascend, intransitively (be high) or actively (mount); used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative
32.kj: arise (up), (cause to) ascend up, at once, break (the day) (up), bring (up), (cause to) burn, carry up, cast up, [phrase] shew, climb (up), (cause to, make to) come (up), cut off,

32.gl: to exist, i.e. be or become, come to pass (always emphatic, and not a mere copula or auxiliary)
32.kj: beacon, [idiom] altogether, be(-come), accomplished, committed, like), break, cause, come (to pass), do, faint, fall, [phrase] follow, happen, [idiom] have, last, pertain, quit (on

32.gl: to erect, i.e. create; by extension, to procure, especially by purchase (causatively, sell); by implication to own
32.kj: attain, buy(-er), teach to keep cattle, get, provoke to jealousy, possess(-or), purchase, recover, redeem, [idiom] surely, [idiom] verily.

32.gl: properly, something inherited, i.e. (abstractly) occupancy, or (concretely) an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion
32.kj: heritage, to inherit, inheritance, possession. Compare H5158 (נַחַל).

32.gl: properly, smoothness; figuratively, flattery; also an allotment
32.kj: field, flattering(-ry), ground, parcel, part, piece of land (ground), plat, portion, slippery place, smooth (thing).

33.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
33.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

33.gl: to give, used with greatest latitude of application (put, make, etc.)
33.kj: add, apply, appoint, ascribe, assign, [idiom] avenge, [idiom] be (healed), bestow, bring (forth, hither), cast, cause, charge, come, commit, consider, count, [phrase] cry, deliver

33.gl: to die (literally or figuratively); causatively, to kill
33.kj: [idiom] at all, [idiom] crying, (be) dead (body, man, one), (put to, worthy of) death, destroy(-er), (cause to, be like to, must) die, kill, necro(-mancer), [idiom] must needs, sla

33.gl: Ephrajim, a son of Joseph; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory
33.dv: dual of masculine form of H672 (אֶפְרָת); double fruit;

33.gl: a mountain or range of hills (sometimes used figuratively)
Pinechas, the name of three Israelites
33.dv: apparently from H6310 (פֶּה) and a variation of H5175 (נָחָשׁ); mouth of a serpent;
